title: Lab: Assemble a Community Media Center
summary: Lab: Assemble a Community Media Center uses an original community media center handoff scenario to retrieve earlier HTML skills, add a bounded artifact layer, and verify transfer with source and browser evidence.
objectives: Retrieve html audio, html video, html links urls without step-by-step imitation.
Produce and verify a real community media center handoff artifact or evidence report.
Preserve earlier valid requirements while diagnosing changed content and constraints.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Build the public information artifact here. -->

steps.title: Triage the community media center handoff · lab
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Which observation identifies the highest-value source boundary to test first? Work context: community media center handoff.
steps.why: Lab: Assemble a Community Media Center requires this decision because each decision must preserve document meaning, user control, portability, and inspectable evidence. This is checkpoint 1 in the community media center handoff.

content.title: community media center handoff · evidence decision
content.text: Commit before feedback, then connect the selected claim to the community media center handoff evidence.

hints: Lab: Assemble a Community Media Center: first isolate the evidence required by “Triage the community media center handoff.”
Lab: Assemble a Community Media Center: compare the current artifact with the named predict goal for step 1.
Lab: Assemble a Community Media Center: use the smallest defensible change or claim that satisfies “Triage the community media center handoff.”

options.text: Community media center handoff case 1: autoplay is required so keyboard users can discover that audio exists.
Community media center handoff case 1: usable audio needs controls, supported sources, fallback, and an equivalent transcript.
Community media center handoff case 1: a one-sentence summary is always equivalent to a detailed emergency advisory.

steps.why: Lab: Assemble a Community Media Center keeps earlier work active while adding one testable HTML increment. This is checkpoint 2 in the community media center handoff.

options.text: Community media center handoff case 3: once an audio file plays, controls, fallback, and a transcript are optional accessibility extras.
Community media center handoff case 3: usable audio needs controls, supported sources, fallback, and an equivalent transcript.
Community media center handoff case 3: a one-sentence summary is always equivalent to a detailed emergency advisory.

steps.title: Reconstruct the community media center handoff root cause · lab
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 5: Arrange the investigation from reproducible symptom to verified repair. Work context: community media center handoff.
steps.why: Lab: Assemble a Community Media Center depends on causal ordering because each decision must preserve document meaning, user control, portability, and inspectable evidence. This is checkpoint 5 in the community media center handoff.

content.text: Reconstruct the community media center handoff sequence by dependency, not by how familiar each term looks.

hints: Lab: Assemble a Community Media Center: first isolate the evidence required by “Reconstruct the community media center handoff root cause.”

options.text: community media center handoff phase 5.1: capture the failing source and observable symptom
community media center handoff phase 5.2: inspect source, DOM, and relevant accessibility evidence
community media center handoff phase 5.3: change the smallest root-cause relationship
community media center handoff phase 5.4: rerun the complete requirement set
